name: BTC Asymmetric Trend Persistence v1
edge_mechanism: continuation with front-loaded exits and structure-based back-half risk
thesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing whether continuation can still work in a mixed transition tape when trigger, risk, and exit indicators have clearly separated roles. The edge is more about management asymmetry than pure trend conviction.
invalidation_condition: Abort if continuation remains too sparse, if front-half exits realize but the back-half structure never pays, or if the regime is too rangy for continuation to survive after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional move stays aligned with fast value long enough for a first target, while the remaining size is managed by a slower structural exit rather than fixed take-profit only.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in mixed transition, some intraday continuations pay the first leg but fail as full trends. Separating trigger, risk, and exit roles may allow harvesting that partial persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best clean lane for this because continuation behavior is more interpretable than on thinner alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the tape is too rotational, the first leg may exist but residual persistence will be too weak to justify continuation framing.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade with density but runners fail, compress the back-half exposure before changing trigger logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Smaller number of entries than the recovery spec, but decent first-target hit rate with mixed runner outcomes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Primary lane plus one cross-check lane show credible density and better-than-random continuation after front-half harvesting.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade output, or continuation expectancy entirely dependent on a tiny number of outliers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because alignment logic is broad

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest lane for testing asymmetric continuation management in a transitional intraday tape.
test_lanes.continue_if: Continuation trades appear with credible density and front-half exits realize consistently.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Signals are too sparse or only first-target scalps exist with no residual structure edge.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest stream of continuations where early harvesting does most of the work.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same management asymmetry generalizes across major intraday lanes.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH shows similar trigger density and comparable first-leg continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Only BTC works, implying asset-specific structure.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but similar asymmetric continuation profile.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the management idea survives on a slower timeframe where structure exits matter more.
test_lanes.continue_if: 4h yields fewer but cleaner continuation trades with viable runner contribution.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The idea only exists in short-term intraday noise.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower frequency but cleaner persistence after first-target harvesting.

long: Exit remaining 25% on close below EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on close above EMA_55 or 5 bars
